governing_requirement: Execute and sign the complete cross-project compatibility, trust, canonicalization, byte/pixel, time, ownership, authority/training-truth, geometry, idempotency, journal, outage, restart, cache, invalidation, rollback and no-fallback matrix over actual producer and consumer bytes, binding both commits and every release/capability/requirements/adoption/test/artifact identity.

dependency_state.disposition: MF-P6-12.05 remains a hard blocker. Frozen schemas, semantic validators, qualification/adoption evidence binding and currency-review integrity receive partial credit; the production matrix cannot pass before all four prerequisite slices are accepted.

preserved_contract_lineage.boundary: These preserved planning/contract identities are not production runtime qualification or adoption evidence.

verified_foundations: The strict qualification bundle binds release, capability, requirements, semantic/core profiles, executor/environment manifests, compatibility checks, installation verification, all three access-mode slices, evidence catalog and a trusted consumer-qualification signature.
Validation byte-verifies production evidence, recomputes check result hashes, requires exact closed checks/slices and binds adoption to the exact executed qualification bundle.

Frozen request/receipt/error/event validators cover ownership, transforms, encoded/decoded artifacts, authority ceilings, training-truth firewall, idempotency, signed journal, unknown-submission reconciliation, invalidation and no-silent-fallback policy.
Release bundle validation closes supplied artifact bytes and rejects unmanifested content; fixture-only evidence cannot escape into production adoption.
Forty-five focused bridge qualification/adoption/journal/currency tests pass and Ruff reports no issues.

focused_validation.pytest_scope: production qualification/adoption byte binding, fixture firewall, capability derivation, release byte closure, ownership/transform identity, error/idempotency, unknown submission, event/invalidation replay, and complete currency-review tests

currency_review_truth_boundary.interpretation: Signature, chain, event, freshness and current-input integrity verify. Policy remains honestly failed and is not relabeled as pass.

confirmed_gaps: All four required release/vertical-slice prerequisites remain blocked and therefore cannot supply accepted production inputs to the matrix.
No persisted trusted-signed production qualification bundle binds the current MaskFactory implementation commit and actual pinned Main runtime commit.
No actual release, capability, requirements, adoption, installed-tree, API/OpenAPI, node, workflow, package, certificate, revocation, cache, rollback or Main execution bytes are closed in one bundle.
The existing production qualification test constructs synthetic temporary records and evidence bytes; it proves validator behavior, not current external-system execution.

No cross-project fault campaign executes unknown/incompatible fields, trust substitution, byte/pixel drift, wrong owner/person/instance, transform replay, idempotency conflict, outage/OOM/timeout, submitted-unknown restart, stale cache, invalidation, rollback and silent-fallback faults through both runtimes.
The signed currency review lacks the live benchmark certificates and distinct tested rollback evidence required for policy pass.
No exact result catalog binds every matrix row to commands, environments, start/end/exit status, stdout/stderr hashes, evidence paths and deterministic pass/fail reasons.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement the producer-owned qualification runner/evidence bundle over accepted 12.01-12.04 outputs and the pinned Main consumer runtime without weakening the frozen v1 contracts or currency-review policy.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five additive paths are absent and clean. They are disjoint from frozen v1 contracts, prepared 12.01-12.04 paths and preserved MF-P6-08.05 changes.
acceptance_criteria: Consume only accepted 12.01-12.04 evidence plus exact current producer/consumer commits and published release/capability/requirements/adoption identities.
Execute every positive and seeded-negative matrix row through actual producer and Main runtimes with exact command/environment/output/evidence hashes and deterministic closed verdicts.

Bind complete compatibility, trust/canonicalization, byte/pixel/time, ownership, authority/training-truth, geometry, idempotency, journal, outage/restart/cache/invalidation/rollback/no-fallback coverage in one strict signed bundle.
Reject fixture evidence, mutable paths, missing/duplicate results, stale/revoked certificates, unresolved bytes, policy failures or any producer/Main disagreement.
Currency-review integrity and policy status remain separate; the matrix cannot relabel the current failed policy as pass.
implementation_packet.next_action: Hold until MF-P6-12.01 through MF-P6-12.04 are implemented/adopted, live role certificates and distinct rollback evidence exist, and exact-project worker routing is qualified.

claim_boundary: This audit credits strict qualification/adoption validators, frozen semantic contracts, evidence-byte binding and truthful currency-review integrity only. It does not execute the production cross-project matrix, publish a signed bundle, prove any vertical slice, satisfy the currency policy, complete prerequisites, or complete MF-P6-12.05.
